01_initial_request.input_data: 242 CT coronary angiograms and plasma samples from patients with acute chest pain (myocardial infarction excluded)
01_initial_request.user_intent: Evaluate the association between cardiac troponin and coronary plaque composition to understand the pathophysiological mechanism for increased cardiovascular risk
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Association (adjusted Odds Ratio) between plasma cardiac troponin I concentration ≥5 ng/L and low-attenuation coronary plaque burden

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-sensitivity cardiac troponin assays are used to rule in/out myocardial infarction, but intermediate concentrations have uncertain pathophysiological mechanisms. [Gap] The underlying mechanism for increased cardiovascular risk in patients with intermediate troponin concentrations remains uncertain. [Decision] To achieve risk stratification, chose to use ARCHITECT STAT Troponin I Assay to perform plasma concentration measurement on patients with acute chest pain, expecting to obtain stratification into low (<5 ng/L) and intermediate (≥5 ng/L) groups.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 161 patients had intermediate concentrations (≥5 ng/L) and 81 had low concentrations (<5 ng/L).

references.relevance: Describes the assay and thresholds used for patient stratification.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CT coronary angiography can define plaque characteristics beyond stenosis. [Gap] Quantitative plaque composition data is needed to evaluate association with troponin levels. [Decision] To achieve imaging of coronary arteries, chose to use Biotech mCT scanner to perform CT coronary angiography on recruited participants, expecting to obtain high-quality coronary angiograms.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 242 scans were of sufficient quality to undertake plaque analysis out of 250 participants.

references.relevance: Details the scanning protocol and equipment used.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Quantitative plaque analysis provides mechanistic insights into prognosis. [Gap] Specific burden of low-attenuation plaque needs to be quantified to test association with troponin. [Decision] To achieve plaque quantification, chose to use AutoPlaque V.2.5 software to perform blinded quantitative plaque analysis on CT scans, expecting to obtain plaque burden metrics for total, non-calcified, calcified, and low-attenuation plaques.

parameters.manufacturer: Cedars-Sinai Medical Center, Los Angeles, USA
parameters.blinding: blinded to patients' results and demographics

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Patients with troponin ≥5 ng/L had higher low-attenuation plaque burden (median 1% vs 0%, p≤0.001).

references.relevance: Validates the software method and observer agreement.
Defines the <30 HU threshold for low-attenuation plaque.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Associations between biomarkers and plaque need adjustment for clinical confounders. [Gap] Independent association between troponin and low-attenuation plaque burden must be statistically verified. [Decision] To achieve statistical evaluation, chose to use R software to perform multivariable logistic regression analysis on clinical and plaque data, expecting to obtain adjusted Odds Ratios and confidence intervals.

parameters.adjustments: age, sex, smoking history, hypertension, diabetes mellitus, eGFR, plaque volume
parameters.transformation: log2 of 1 plus the plaque variable

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Low-attenuation plaque burden was independently associated with troponin ≥5 ng/L (adjusted OR per doubling 1.62, 95% CI 1.17 to 2.32, p=0.005).

references.relevance: Describes the regression models and software used.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multivariable logistic regression analysis adjusting for clinical risk factors and coronary artery disease presence

adjusted_odds_ratio.interpretation: Patients with troponin ≥5 ng/L have 1.62 times higher odds of low-attenuation plaque burden per doubling, independent of clinical characteristics.

confidence_interval.interpretation: The association is statistically significant as the interval does not include 1.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully evaluated the association between cardiac troponin and coronary plaque composition, finding a significant independent association between plasma cardiac troponin I concentration ≥5 ng/L and low-attenuation coronary plaque burden.
